subject: [PATCH v5 5/6] nfsd: count NFSv4 callback operations per netns

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer Chuck Lever noted that the patch introduces a use-after-free (UAF) possibility, specifically in nfsd4_run_cb(), which increments the per-netns callback counter after freeing the callback structure, and requested addressing this issue before applying the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
I agree with the Sashiko review that this patch introduces a UAF
possibility that needs to be addressed before the patch is applied.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-06-18.patch_summary: This patch adds per-netns counters to track NFSv4 callback operations in the Linux kernel's NFS server implementation. It introduces a new array of percpu counters for callback operations, indexed by RFC 8881 callback opcode, and updates the nfsd4_run_cb() function to bump the relevant counter for each queued callback. The patch also removes redundant statistics tracking for wdeleg_getattr conflicts and drops related code. This change aims to provide more accurate statistics for NFSv4 backchannel operations.
